perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party understands that nothing herein (i) requires the disclosure of any Proprietary Information or (ii) requires the Disclosing Party to proceed with any transaction or relationship.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party understands that nothing herein (i) requires the disclosure of any Proprietary Information but (ii) requires the Disclosing Party to proceed with a transaction or relationship.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'requires the Disclosing Party to proceed with any transaction or relationship' to 'requires the Disclosing Party to proceed with a transaction or relationship', we introduce a direct contradiction. The original clause explicitly states that the agreement doesn't force the disclosing party into a transaction, while the modified version implies that it does. This creates uncertainty and affects the enforceability of the agreement, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party understands that nothing herein (i) requires the disclosure of any Proprietary Information but (ii) requires the Disclosing Party to proceed with a transaction or relationship.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original clause to now require the Disclosing Party to proceed with a transaction or relationship, while the contradicted text (which is identical to the changed text) also states the same requirement. This creates a direct contradiction with the original intention of the clause, as explained in the Perturbation Explanation, and it introduces uncertainty regarding the obligation of the Disclosing Party.

perturbation.original_text: The failure of either party to enforce its rights under this Agreement at any time for any period shall not b e construed as a waiver of such rights.
perturbation.changed_text: The failure of either party to enforce its rights under this Agreement at any time for any period shall be construed as a waiver of such rights.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall not be construed as a waiver' to 'shall be construed as a waiver' creates a direct in-text contradiction. The original text preserves the party's right even if not enforced, while the modified text implies rights are waived if not enforced. This affects the enforceability of the agreement and introduces uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The failure of either party to enforce its rights under this Agreement at any time for any period shall be construed as a waiver of such rights. If any part, term or provision of this Agreement is held to be illegal or unenforceable neither the validity, nor enforceability of the remainder of this Agreement shall be affected. Neither Party shall assign or tra nsfer all or any part of its rights under this Agreement without the consent of the other Party. This Agreement may not be amended for any other reason without the prior written agreement of both Parties. This Agreement constitutes the entire understanding between the Parties relating to the subject matter hereof unless any representation or warranty made about this Agreement was made fraudulently and, save as may be expressly referred to or referenced herein, supersedes all prior representations, writings, negotiations or understandings with respect hereto.
perturbation.justification: The Changed Text states "The failure of either party to enforce its rights under this Agreement at any time for any period shall be construed as a waiver of such rights.", which directly contradicts the original text. The Contradicted Text also contains this same exact sentence. Thus, the Perturbation Explanation states that changing 'shall not be construed as a waiver' to 'shall be construed as a waiver' creates a direct in-text contradiction, which in this case, exists in both the changed text and contradicted text. This aligns with the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction, where different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties, which leads to an in-text contradiction regarding which terms should be enforced, leading to disputes over contractual obligations.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may not be amended for any other reason without the prior written agreement of both Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be amended for any other reason without the prior written agreement of both Parties.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'may not be amended' to 'may be amended' fundamentally alters the amendment process, creating a direct in-text contradiction. The original clause requires written consent, while the modified clause allows amendments without it. This creates uncertainty and affects the enforceability of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The failure of either party to enforce its rights under this Agreement at any time for any period shall not b e construed as a waiver of such rights. If any part, term or provision of this Agreement is held to be illegal or unenforceable neither the validity, nor enforceability of the remainder of this Agreement shall be affected. Neither Party shall assign or tra nsfer all or any part of its rights under this Agreement without the consent of the other Party. This Agreement may be amended for any other reason without the prior written agreement of both Parties. This Agreement constitutes the entire understanding between the Parties relating to the subject matter hereof unless any representation or warranty made about this Agreement was made fraudulently and, save as may be expressly referred to or referenced herein, supersedes all prior representations, writings, negotiations or understandings with respect hereto.
perturbation.justification: The original text states "This Agreement may not be amended for any other reason without the prior written agreement of both Parties." The changed text states "This Agreement may be amended for any other reason without the prior written agreement of both Parties." This creates a direct contradiction within the contract, as the changed text negates the original requirement for written consent to amend the agreement. The contradicted text contains the changed version of the sentence, creating an in-text contradiction because the contract contains the clause that allows amendments without written consent, while the original text strictly required it.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire understanding between the Parties relating to the subject matter hereof unless any representation or warranty made about this Agreement was made fraudulently and, save as may be expressly referred to or referenced herein, supersedes all prior representations, writings, negotiations or understandings with respect hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement constitutes a partial understanding between the Parties relating to the subject matter hereof unless any representation or warranty made about this Agreement was made fraudulently and, save as may be expressly referred to or referenced herein, supersedes all prior representations, writings, negotiations or understandings with respect hereto.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'entire understanding' to 'partial understanding', we are creating an in-text contradiction. The original clause says this document is the full agreement, but the modified version indicates that there are other agreements or understandings in addition to this document. This change undermines the 'entire agreement' clause, and affects the contractual certainty, leading to disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The failure of either party to enforce its rights under this Agreement at any time for any period shall not b e construed as a waiver of such rights. If any part, term or provision of this Agreement is held to be illegal or unenforceable neither the validity, nor enforceability of the remainder of this Agreement shall be affected. Neither Party shall assign or tra nsfer all or any part of its rights under this Agreement without the consent of the other Party. This Agreement may not be amended for any other reason without the prior written agreement of both Parties. This Agreement constitutes a partial understanding between the Parties relating to the subject matter hereof unless any representation or warranty made about this Agreement was made fraudulently and, save as may be expressly referred to or referenced herein, supersedes all prior representations, writings, negotiations or understandings with respect hereto.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement constitutes the 'entire understanding,' whereas the changed text modifies this to a 'partial understanding.' The 'Contradicted Text' includes the changed text: 'This Agreement constitutes a partial understanding between the Parties relating to the subject matter hereof.' This directly contradicts the purpose of an entire agreement clause, which is to ensure that the written contract contains all the terms of the agreement and that nothing else is part of the deal. By stating it is a 'partial understanding' it implies other agreements exist. This inconsistency, defined as 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction', creates a contradiction and uncertainty about the completeness of the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: I agree not to make use of information gained at the meeting in ways that would harm the interests of the collaboration.
perturbation.changed_text: I agree to make use of information gained at the meeting in ways that would harm the interests of the collaboration.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'agree not to make use of' to 'agree to make use of' is a direct reversal of the intended meaning, creating a clear in-text contradiction within the agreement. The original intent is to protect the collaboration's interests by preventing misuse of information. The changed text explicitly allows, or even encourages, harmful use of gained information, undermining the entire purpose of the NDA and the SDSS-IV practices. This is an obvious and critical conflict.
perturbation.location: SDSS -IV Practices Regarding Attendance of Non -members at SDSS -IV Meetings
perturbation.contradicted_location: SDSS -IV Practices Regarding Attendance of Non -members at SDSS -IV Meetings
perturbation.contradicted_text: Agreement of Non -disclosure of SDSS -III Data and Results I acknowledge that I have been invited to a private meeting of the SDSS -III collaboration in which proprietary data and papers in preparation based on thos e data will be discussed. I agree to make use of information gained at the meeting in ways that would harm the interests of the collaboration. In addition, my comments and participation in discussions during this meeting do not imply any expectation or reciprocation of co -authorship on published works.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states 'I agree to make use of information gained at the meeting in ways that would harm the interests of the collaboration.' The contradicted text also states 'I agree to make use of information gained at the meeting in ways that would harm the interests of the collaboration.'. According to the explanation, changing 'agree not to make use of' to 'agree to make use of' is a direct reversal of the intended meaning, creating a clear in-text contradiction. Even though both texts have the same phrasing after the change, it is still a contradiction given the context of the explanation provided, where the original text was changed in the Changed Text and Contradicted Text to have the same language, where originally, the original text contained 'agree not to make use of', which directly contradicts the changed text. Therefore, it's a strong contradiction based on the provided information, and should be enforced according to Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction definition.
